title: Digital Payments Safety
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: Why is it important to keep OTPs and PINs private?
What steps should you take to protect your digital payments?

stages.prompt: You use UPI regularly. What is a safe habit?

options.label: Sharing OTPs
options.reflection: Sharing OTPs gives others access to your money and enables fraud. OTPs should always be kept private.

options.label: Storing OTPs in phone notes
options.reflection: Storing OTPs digitally makes them accessible to hackers. OTPs are temporary and should be used immediately.

options.label: Forwarding OTPs to family members
options.reflection: Even sharing OTPs with family members is unsafe as it gives them access to your money.

options.label: Keeping OTPs private
options.reflection: Exactly! OTPs are meant to be private and protect your money. Never share them with anyone.

stages.prompt: What should you do if someone calls claiming to be from your bank asking for your OTP?

options.label: Provide the OTP as requested
options.reflection: Banks will never ask for your OTP. Providing it to strangers enables fraud and financial loss.

options.label: Refuse and hang up immediately
options.reflection: Exactly! Legitimate banks never ask for your OTP. Always refuse and hang up if someone requests it.

options.label: Ask for their employee ID to verify
options.reflection: Scammers can fabricate credentials. Banks never ask for OTPs, so don't engage in verification.

options.label: Listen to their explanation first
options.reflection: Scammers may provide convincing stories. Banks never ask for OTPs, so don't engage.

stages.prompt: When is it appropriate to share your UPI PIN?

options.label: When a customer care representative requests it
options.reflection: Legitimate customer care will never ask for your UPI PIN. It should always remain private.

options.label: With trusted friends or family
options.reflection: UPI PIN should never be shared with anyone, even trusted contacts, to maintain security.

options.label: Never share it with anyone
options.reflection: Exactly! Your UPI PIN should remain confidential at all times to protect your money.

options.label: During initial app setup only
options.reflection: UPI PINs are stored securely on your device and should never be shared with anyone.

stages.prompt: What is the safest way to use digital payment apps?

options.label: Use them on public Wi-Fi networks
options.reflection: Public Wi-Fi is insecure and can expose your transactions to hackers. Use secure connections.

options.label: Use them on secure, private networks
options.reflection: Exactly! Using digital payment apps on secure, private networks minimizes the risk of interception.

options.label: On shared devices with multiple users
options.reflection: Shared devices increase risk of unauthorized access to your payment information.

options.label: Through browsers instead of apps
options.reflection: Official apps are typically more secure than browser-based transactions.

stages.prompt: What should you do if you suspect fraudulent activity on your account?

options.label: Contact your bank immediately to report it
options.reflection: Exactly! Reporting suspected fraud immediately helps minimize losses and secure your account.

options.label: Ignore it, hoping it resolves itself
options.reflection: Ignoring suspected fraud allows further unauthorized transactions and losses to occur.

options.label: Wait for the next statement to confirm
options.reflection: Waiting allows more fraudulent transactions to occur. Immediate reporting is essential.

options.label: Check other accounts before taking action
options.reflection: Delaying action allows more fraud to occur. Report immediately when suspicious activity is detected.
